title: Do not default to the four-column footer
summary: AI-generated footers universally use four columns with Product, Resources, Company, Legal headings, a social icon row, and a copyright bar — regardless of the site's actual link density.
instruction: Scale the footer to the site's actual information needs. Consider a single-column copyright-only footer, a two-column layout, or a sitemap-style footer for content-heavy sites. Reserve multi-column footers for sites with 12+ genuine destination links.
rationale: The four-column footer is an AI template pattern that wastes vertical space and adds cognitive load. Users rarely scan footer links, so the link-dense footer provides poor return on visual real estate. Screen readers must navigate all links, compounding accessibility cost.
violations: Exactly four columns regardless of actual link count
Column headings: Product, Resources, Company, Legal (or close variants)
Circular social media icon buttons in a horizontal row
Copyright bar with thin separator containing © symbol and company name
Same footer link structure regardless of brand or industry
controls: Minimal footer with copyright and essential legal links only
Two-column footer for moderate link density
Sitemap-style footer for content-rich editorial sites
Footer integrated into the page design as a content element rather than a template block
borderlineCases: Large corporate sites with legal mandates to list many links
contextualExceptions: Documentation sites where footer serves as secondary navigation
detectorSignal: A bottom-of-page section with 3-5 columns of text links, social media icons, and a copyright notice.
matcherProposal: Detect a page-bottom section containing multiple columned link lists with headings (Product, Resources, etc.) and social media icon links.
detectorLimitations: Cannot assess if link density justifies the column layout
falsePositiveScenarios: Large corporate sites with actual legal requirements for extensive footers
mitigationStrategy: Flag as a low-priority suggestion, noting the pattern frequency in AI-generated sites.

fixturePlans.violation: Four-column footer with Product, Resources, Company, Legal columns, social icons, copyright bar.
fixturePlans.control: Minimal footer: copyright line only, no social icons, no link columns.
fixturePlans.borderline: E-commerce footer with genuine need for multiple link categories.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
